stage_routing.name: QualiTrace_AI_StageRouting
stage_routing.description: Defines all 7 Maestro stages and the routing conditions between them. All field name references use exact snake_case from CaseDefinition.json. Global pause rules apply before any per-stage routing is evaluated.

global_pause_rules.description: These two rules are evaluated FIRST at every stage transition, before any stage-specific routing condition is checked. If either rule fires, routing halts immediately.

rules.name: MissingBatchNumber_Pause
rules.description: If batch_number is null at any point in the workflow, case status is set to pending_review and routing pauses. The case does not move forward or backward. When batch_number is supplied via the update endpoint, routing resumes from the exact stage where it paused.

rules.name: LowConfidence_MandatoryHumanReview
rules.description: If confidence_score is below 0.6, the case is routed to Stage 4 HumanReview regardless of risk_level or current stage. This rule takes precedence over all per-stage routing conditions except GPR-001 (which is evaluated first).

evaluation_order: GPR-001 evaluated first — if fires, routing stops (no further evaluation)
GPR-002 evaluated second — if fires, route to Stage4_HumanReview (skip stage-specific rules)
Stage-specific routing conditions evaluated only if both GPR-001 and GPR-002 do not fire

stages.name: Intake
stages.description: Initial complaint receipt and field extraction. The Intake Agent parses the incoming complaint, extracts all Golden Rule fields, assigns risk_level and confidence_score, and sets the initial status.

entry_conditions.description: Entry point for all new complaints. No prior stage required.

actions: Extract and validate all Golden Rule fields from the complaint source
Assign case_id in C-### format
Set initial status to 'open'
Invoke Intake Agent to assign risk_level and confidence_score
Record intake event in audit trail

routing_out.description: Intake always transitions to Stage 2. There are no branch conditions at this stage — all cases proceed.

stages.notes: Global pause rules are still evaluated here. If batch_number is null after intake, case pauses at Stage 1 before advancing to Stage 2.

stages.name: RiskAssessment
stages.description: Evaluates risk_level assigned by the Intake Agent and routes accordingly. This is the first fork in the workflow. Critical cases bypass investigation entirely and go directly to human review.
actions: Read risk_level from case record
Read confidence_score from case record
Apply global pause rules before routing
Evaluate routing conditions in priority order

routing_out.description: CRITICAL FAST-TRACK: risk_level 'critical' routes immediately to Stage 4 HumanReview. Stage 3 AutoInvestigation is skipped entirely. This rule is evaluated before all other Stage 2 routing conditions.
routing_out.note: risk_level has exactly 4 values: low, medium, high, critical. This condition matches only 'critical' — not 'high'.

routing_out.description: High risk proceeds to automated investigation. Note: if confidence_score < 0.6 also applies, GPR-002 fires first and overrides this routing.
Medium risk proceeds to automated investigation.
Low risk proceeds to automated investigation.

stages.notes: risk_level must be one of exactly four values: low, medium, high, critical. Do not collapse high and critical — they have different routing behaviour. GPR-002 is still active: even a low/medium/high case routes to HumanReview if confidence_score < 0.6.

stages.name: AutoInvestigation
stages.description: AI-driven investigation using the RAG pipeline. The Investigation Agent retrieves jurisdiction-filtered documents, builds hypotheses, and returns confidence_score and source_list. This stage is skipped for critical risk cases.
actions: Invoke RAG retriever with jurisdiction-filtered knowledge base
Invoke Investigation Agent with RAG context
Validate agent output (3 hypotheses, source_ids present, overall_confidence in range)
Update confidence_score from agent output
Populate source_list from retrieved chunks
Record investigation output in audit trail

routing_out.description: Low confidence score means the AI is not sufficiently certain of its findings. Case must go to human review before any CAPA is generated.
routing_out.note: This condition mirrors GPR-002 at the stage level. GPR-002 would already have fired if confidence_score was below 0.6 before this stage; this condition catches cases where confidence_score drops below 0.6 as a result of the investigation itself.

routing_out.description: Sufficient confidence — AI investigation is accepted and CAPA generation can proceed.

stages.notes: Agent output must be validated by output_validator.py before this stage advances. If validation fails after 3 retries, route to Stage4_HumanReview with task_type = risk_review.

stages.name: HumanReview
stages.description: Mandatory human decision point. Triggered by: (a) risk_level == 'critical' from Stage 2, (b) confidence_score < 0.6 from Stage 2 or Stage 3, or (c) agent validation failure. The assigned reviewer sees the AI recommendation, confidence_score, and full source_list before making a decision.

requires: One of: risk_level == 'critical' OR confidence_score < 0.6 OR agent_validation_failed == true
Human task record created with correct task_type and SLA deadline

actions: Create human task with assigned role: quality_lead
Set SLA deadline based on risk_level (see SLAConfig.json)
Present AI recommendation, confidence_score, and source_list to reviewer
Await human decision: approve, reject, or override
If decision == 'override': require and record override_reason
Record human decision and override_reason (if present) in audit trail
Set human_approval_flag = true if decision is approve or override

routing_out.description: Reviewer approves the AI recommendation. Case advances to CAPA generation.
Reviewer overrides the AI recommendation with their own judgement. override_reason is mandatory — routing is blocked until a non-empty override_reason is provided. Case then advances to CAPA.
Reviewer rejects the AI findings. Case routes back to Stage 3 for a fresh investigation. human_approval_flag is NOT set.

blocking_rules.message: override_reason is required when decision is 'override'. Routing is blocked until a non-empty override_reason is recorded.

stages.name: CAPA
stages.description: Corrective and Preventive Action generation. The CAPA Agent produces a structured action plan based on the approved root cause. Every CAPA action must cite at least one source from source_list.

requires: Stage4_HumanReview completed with decision IN ['approve', 'override'] OR Stage3_AutoInvestigation completed with confidence_score >= 0.6

actions: Invoke CAPA Agent with approved root cause, investigation output, and jurisdiction
Validate every CAPA action has at least one source citation from source_list
Store CAPA plan linked to case_id
Set CAPA SLA deadlines per action (48 hours default — see SLAConfig.json)
Record CAPA generation event in audit trail

routing_out.description: Valid CAPA plan generated with all citations present. Routes to effectiveness review.

stages.notes: If CAPA Agent fails to produce a valid plan with citations after 3 retries, escalate to Stage4_HumanReview with task_type = capa_approval.

stages.name: EffectivenessReview
stages.description: Evaluates whether all CAPA actions have been completed and are effective. A human reviewer confirms whether the corrective actions resolved the root cause. Cannot be triggered before all CAPA actions are marked complete.

requires: All CAPA action items marked complete

actions: Confirm all CAPA actions are in status: complete
Create human effectiveness review task with task_type: capa_approval
Record effectiveness decision and evidence in audit trail

side_effects: status set to 'open' pending final closure step
routing_out.description: All CAPA actions are effective. Case advances to closure.
CAPA actions did not resolve the root cause. Case routes back to Stage 5 for a revised CAPA plan.

stages.name: Closure
stages.description: Final stage. Case is closed and the closure report is generated. This stage is a HARD BLOCK — it cannot be entered unless human_approval_flag is true. This is not a warning; routing into this stage with human_approval_flag == false is a blocking error.

hard_block.message: HARD BLOCK: Case cannot enter Closure stage. human_approval_flag is false. A human reviewer must formally approve the case before closure is permitted. This block cannot be bypassed by any system action.

actions: Verify human_approval_flag == true (hard block if false — not a warning)
Set status to 'closed'
Trigger ReportGenerator.xaml to produce closure PDF
Record closure timestamp and approving actor in audit trail
Audit event tagged: CASE_CLOSED
stages.notes: This is a terminal stage. No outbound routing. The only exit from a closed case is via a new complaint submission that references the original case_id.

stage_map_summary.description: Quick reference for all routing paths.
paths: Stage5 → Stage6 (after valid CAPA plan generated)

field_references.note: All field names below are the exact snake_case identifiers from CaseDefinition.json. Do not rename or reformat them.
